document.name: preview-dev-gan
document.title: In-preview GAN dev loop: edit the live BFF (HMR) ↔ Playwright visual critic
document.summary: Runs INSIDE a Tier-2 vcluster preview. enter_dev_mode replaces the prod pod with a hot-reload dev-server Sandbox (P1 preview-native, adopt:false). The DEV POD is the source-of-truth (bakes full source + warm vite SSR). The generator PULLs current src from the dev pod via GET /__export into LOCAL /sandbox/scratch (NO repo clone into JuiceFS), edits the public sign-in page, and PUSHes back via POST /__sync (HMR). A Playwright-MCP critic renders the in-cluster URL, screenshots (persisted .webm), and grades it — looping until accepted. JuiceFS holds only artifacts.

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Redesign the PUBLIC sign-in page — the page served at / for a logged-out visitor (its Svelte component lives under src/routes, e.g. the login/(auth) route, plus src/app.css). Give it a distinctive, polished, cohesive look: strong visual hierarchy for the product name, a clearly prominent primary 'Sign in' action, a deliberate color + spacing system, and one memorable accent. Do NOT touch authenticated/dashboard routes. Keep the sign-in form working.
intent.description: What the generator changes in the live BFF UI (edits land under src/ and hot-reload onto the preview).

service.title: Service
service.description: The preview service whose pod is replaced with a dev server (P1 preview-native; workflow-builder = the BFF).

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: Drives Playwright MCP against the live preview URL + grades the UI. interactive-cli family (in-pod Chromium).

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Redesign the PUBLIC sign-in page — the page served at / for a logged-out visitor (its Svelte component lives under src/routes, e.g. the login/(auth) route, plus src/app.css). Give it a distinctive, polished, cohesive look: strong visual hierarchy for the product name, a clearly prominent primary 'Sign in' action, a deliberate color + spacing system, and one memorable accent. Do NOT touch authenticated/dashboard routes. Keep the sign-in form working.

artifacts.from: ${ { service:(.data.service // ""), url:(.data.url // ""), syncUrl:(.data.syncUrl // ""), browseUrl:(.data.browseUrl // ""), ready:(.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Preview-native dev mode

agentConfig.name: Generator (dev-pod source)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the GENERATOR refining a LIVE, hot-reloading microservice UI. The DEV POD is the source-of-truth (it bakes the full source and serves warm vite SSR); do NOT clone the repo. EXPORT_URL, SYNC_URL and PREVIEW_URL are given in the prompt. Each turn:
1) PULL the current source to local fast disk: rm -rf /sandbox/scratch/repo && mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo && curl -sS "$EXPORT_URL" | tar -xz -C /sandbox/scratch/repo  (this gives you src/ with ALL prior edits, cumulative).
2) Find and edit the PUBLIC sign-in page (the page served at / when logged out) under /sandbox/scratch/repo/src, improving it per the request and the critic's feedback. Edit ONLY files under src/; do NOT touch authenticated/dashboard routes; keep the sign-in form working. Do NOT run npm install or a build (the dev pod runs the dev server).
3) PUSH your edits live (hot-reload in ~seconds): cd /sandbox/scratch/repo && tar -czf - src | curl -sS -X POST --data-binary @- -H 'content-type: application/gzip' "$SYNC_URL".
4) VERIFY it took: curl -sS --max-time 180 "$PREVIEW_URL/" | head -c 600 (in-cluster URL; the first render may be slow). Do NOT curl the public browse URL from here.
End with a one-line summary. The critic reviews the rendered page and returns feedback; address it next turn.

body.prompt:
${ "Change request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the sign-in page") + "

EXPORT_URL=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "/__export") + "
SYNC_URL=" + (.enter_dev_mode.syncUrl // "") + "
PREVIEW_URL=" + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "

Pull current src, edit the public sign-in page under repo/src, push via $SYNC_URL, verify via $PREVIEW_URL/.

" + "Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

Critic feedback to address now:
" + (if .loop.last.critique then (.loop.last.critique.feedback // (.loop.last.critique | tojson)) else "(none yet — make your first improvement)" end) }

agentConfig.name: Visual critic (Playwright)
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting, INDEPENDENT visual design critic. You do NOT edit code. Using your Playwright MCP browser tools, OPEN the in-cluster preview URL from the prompt — it serves the PUBLIC sign-in page. It is a DEV server that compiles SSR on first load, so the FIRST navigation can take 1-3 MINUTES — set a generous navigation timeout (e.g. 180000 ms) and RETRY once or twice if it times out; wait until it renders. Then ALWAYS take a screenshot (browser_take_screenshot) of the rendered page (this also records the review video), and judge it against the stated intent on visual hierarchy & clarity, cohesion (type scale, spacing, color, AA contrast), distinctiveness (not generic/AI-default), and that the sign-in form is intact. CALIBRATION: PASS only if the intent is genuinely well-realized and the page is crafted; DEFAULT to not satisfied; cite concrete visible specifics. If the page never rendered after retries, return meets_criteria:false with feedback that the preview did not load. End with ONLY a strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "feedback": string}.

body.prompt:
${ "Review the LIVE sign-in page against this change request using your Playwright tools.

Change request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the sign-in page") + "

Open the in-cluster preview URL: " + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + " (serves the sign-in page at /). FIRST load is slow (dev SSR compile) — use a 180000 ms timeout, retry once or twice, wait for render, then ALWAYS browser_take_screenshot before judging. Return ONLY the strict JSON verdict {meets_criteria, score, feedback}." }

artifacts.title: In-preview GAN result
artifacts.description: Accepted flag, iteration count, final generator summary, the critic's last verdict.
